newsSince: No new fundamental news since June 9; the binary catalyst is the June 25 AGM. ISS and Glass Lewis back Oasis (remove Chairman Yamaguchi, appoint Okamura, JPY350B buyback); the board unanimously opposes. Outcome unknown as of June 23. Stock essentially flat (-0.94%).

sources.label: ISS/Glass Lewis back Oasis at Kyocera (Yahoo/Oasis)
Kyocera board rejects Oasis proposals ahead of AGM (TipRanks)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha/passives lens: tantalum/ceramic/specialty-capacitor bottleneck thesis unchanged. The ~28% leg is event-driven activist/AGM flow (ISS/Glass Lewis backing Oasis), not new passives evidence; June 25 vote is binary and unresolved. Hold at 0.
verdicts.bull: AVX-tight tantalum/ceramic/specialty-cap bottlenecks plus an activist catalyst that could unlock capital return.
verdicts.neutral: Recent leg is governance-event flow, not passives-specific evidence; AGM outcome pending.
verdicts.bear: Event-driven alpha is being consumed into June 25; vote could disappoint and reverse.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 25 AGM: Oasis buyback/board proposals with proxy-advisor support.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AGM outcome binary; failed vote unwinds event premium.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master bottleneck lens: nothing since June 9 changes Kyocera's role in AI-server PDN, ceramic packages and substrates. Proxy-advisor backing for the activist slate modestly improves the capital-allocation overhang, but the June 25 vote is unresolved. Hold at 0.
verdicts.bull: AI-server ceramic packages, substrates and MLCC/inductor content plus potential governance-driven capital reform.
verdicts.neutral: Capital-allocation overhang partly addressed by activist pressure; vote still pending.
verdicts.bear: Conglomerate discount persists if board prevails; measured operational growth.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AGM-driven capital-allocation/governance change; AI-server component demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Activist push fails; capital-policy overhang reasserts.
